verbGlossary.SEGMENTS: {source} subdivides {target}/safety metric for comparative analysis (e.g., project type segments observation density to compare hospitals vs. offices)
verbGlossary.ELEVATES RISK FOR: {source} increases the likelihood or severity of {target} or quality finding (e.g., occupied-during-construction elevates risk for safety observations)
verbGlossary.SCOPES: {source} defines the required scope of {target}, test, or inspection program (e.g., climate zone scopes environmental commissioning observations)
verbGlossary.MANDATES: {source} requires the existence of {target} or observation program (e.g., fire sprinkler requirement mandates inspections, LEED certification mandates commissioning observations)
verbGlossary.DETERMINES: {source} dictates which specific target test reports or special inspections are required (e.g., structural system determines material test reports)
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED AGAINST: {source}-level performance attribute provides the baseline for comparison with {target}/safety metric (e.g., safety incident rate benchmarked against incident records)
verbGlossary.CORRELATED WITH: {source} shows statistical relationship to {target} or quality metric (e.g., peak workforce correlated with hazard recurrence rate)
verbGlossary.SHIFTS FOCUS OF: {source} attribute redirects quality emphasis toward {target} type (e.g., modular/prefab shifts focus to factory QC quality observations)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} progress attribute activates {target} activity at a specific threshold (e.g., percent complete triggers punch item generation at substantial completion)
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {source} outcome is quantified by the volume and trends in {target} records -- more proactive {target} activity indicates better {source}
verbGlossary.INDICATED BY: {source} outcome level is reflected in {target} volume -- high {target} counts in late phases indicate {source} was insufficient earlier
